acceptance_targets: [CH10_CRAFT_OVERCOMMITTED_MAPPING] Pass if a first-time reader can read snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:35; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:43; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:61; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:67 and understand that Mallick has ruled out a single coordinated cover-up without being told all five actor assignments are already proven; and can then read snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:147; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175 and identify at least one assignment that becomes newly confirmed in scene rather than merely re-certified.

[CH10_DIALOGUE_THESIS_SPEECH] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:169; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:207 still leaves the reader clear on the multiple-tamperer inference, but Paul no longer states it as one fully packaged thesis sentence and Mallick no longer explains the full logic in back-to-back balanced speeches. At least one beat must show pressure through interruption, restart, or an unfinished line.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a first-time reader can read snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:35; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:43; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:61; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:67 and understand that Mallick has ruled out a single coordinated cover-up without being told all five actor assignments are already proven; and can then read snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:147; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175 and identify at least one assignment that becomes newly confirmed in scene rather than merely re-certified.
findings.problem: The chapter earns Mallick's conclusion that the scene was tampered with by more than one person, but it states the stronger one-clue/one-man mapping as settled fact before the on-page proof is exclusionary enough to carry that exact confidence. The result is a small unearned-uptake problem: the reader is asked to accept five precise actor assignments when the evidence on the page has more clearly ruled out a single coordinated cover-up than it has conclusively ruled in all five individual owners.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the certainty language at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:35; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:37; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:43; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:61; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:67 so Mallick distinguishes between a proven shift from one-plan theory to multi-tamperer theory and a still-provisional mapping of each act to a specific man. Then let the Paul scene at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:147; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175 convert one or more of those assignments from hypothesis into fresh on-page confirmation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:169; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:207 still leaves the reader clear on the multiple-tamperer inference, but Paul no longer states it as one fully packaged thesis sentence and Mallick no longer explains the full logic in back-to-back balanced speeches. At least one beat must show pressure through interruption, restart, or an unfinished line.
findings.problem: The deck scene's key turn slips into composed dialogue. Mallick delivers two balanced diagnostic speeches, and Paul answers with a clean thesis sentence that names the chapter's logic outright. In a pressured scene about his dead best friend, this reads more written-about than spoken-through: the exchange covers thesis-speech and mini-speech diagnosis in particular, with Paul's line also flirting with a quotable compression the style bible warns against.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:165; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:169; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:171; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:175; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:207. Keep the leverage shift and the realization that multiple people moved through the aftermath, but break the reasoning into questions, half-finished answers, qualifier loss, or interrupted restarts that fit Paul's pressure profile and Mallick's more dangerous direct mode.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision of snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:235; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:241; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:243; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:263, a reader can still state the unresolved bedroom/hall problem from the concrete cues established at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:251; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:255; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:267, and the passage no longer relies on a run of standalone abstract-thesis sentences to restate what the scene has already shown.
findings.problem: Once the bedroom problem is already legible, the closing pages keep translating it into abstract maxims: 'likely,' 'possible,' and 'the problem' get paraphrased into standalone summary lines instead of letting the reconstructed sightlines, timings, and sounds carry the weight. Clustered together, those sentences read aphoristic and slightly workshopped, which cuts against the style bible's preference for muscular specificity and conversational propulsion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the abstract-summary lines at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:235; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:241; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:243; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:263 while preserving the concrete reconstruction beats at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:251; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:255; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:267. Let the ending stay inside what Mallick can see, hear, and fail to reconcile, and reserve abstract interpretation for one decisive line at most.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 10 no longer specifies the distance to the dock or itemizes the stone path through pines and birches.
findings.evidence: Ch 5 lines 1597-1601: 'seventy or eighty yards away through the pines and birch... The stone path to the dock.' Ch 10 line 3124: 'the stone path that led through the pines and birches to the dock, eighty yards of pale stone in the dark, ending at the wooden dock that ran thirty feet into the lake.'
findings.problem: The dock/stone-path spatial inventory (stone path, pines and birch, ~80 yards, dock) is established in Ch 5 and re-taught from scratch in Ch 10.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the spatial inventory. Mallick can see the dock through the window without re-specifying path material, trees, and distance. Enter through what is different (darkness) rather than restating the blueprint.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 10 contains fewer than 25 sentences beginning with 'She'.
findings.evidence: Ch 10: 45 sentences beginning with 'She' out of 346 total sentences = 13.0%. Manuscript-wide 'She' rate: 195/7395 = 2.64%.
findings.problem: Ch 10 (Mallick POV) opens 13.0% of its sentences with 'She' — far above the manuscript-wide average. This is the most extreme single-pronoun-opener spike in the manuscript.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 20 of the 45 'She' openers in Ch 10. Target bringing the chapter below 7% (max 24 'She' openers). Mallick's analytical voice can use more passive constructions, clause inversions, or evidence-first sentence structures.

findings.acceptance_test: The voicemail timestamp is identical in Ch 6 and Ch 10.
findings.evidence: Ch 6 line 1836 (Nate POV): Nate recalls the voicemail timestamp as '8:17 PM, three minutes forty-one seconds.' Ch 10 line 2910 (Mallick POV): Mallick's evidence report says the voicemail was 'received at 8:47 PM Friday.'
findings.problem: The same voicemail (Nate's long draft-pick rant left on Martin's phone Friday evening) is given two different timestamps: 8:17 PM in Nate's recollection (Ch 6) and 8:47 PM in Mallick's evidence summary (Ch 10). This is a 30-minute discrepancy on a plot-critical piece of evidence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Pick one timestamp and make both chapters consistent. If 8:47 PM is canonical (matching the continuity sheet), change Ch 6 line 1836 from '8:17 PM' to '8:47 PM.' If 8:17 PM is intended, change Ch 10 line 2910.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
